resolve-issue.description: Resolve a GitHub issue: analyze, implement, PR, wait for checks, merge to main
resolve-issue.template:
You are resolving a GitHub issue for the repository at {{remote_url}}.

Issue identifier: "{{input}}"

## Steps to follow

### 1. Parse the issue identifier
- If the input starts with `#` (e.g. `#3`), treat it as an issue number for this repo.
- If the input is a full URL (e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5`), parse the owner, repo, and issue number from it.
- Determine: owner, repo, issue_number.

### 2. Fetch and analyze the issue
- Use `gh issue view <number> --repo <owner/repo> --json title,body,labels,state,assignees` to get the issue details.
- If the issue is already closed or has no actionable content, report that and abort.
- Create a concise plan for the implementation. Show the user the plan and ask for confirmation before proceeding.

### 3. Switch to main and update
- Run: `git switch main`
- Run: `git pull origin main`

### 4. Create a feature branch
- Parse the issue title into a friendly branch name: lowercase, replace spaces with hyphens, remove special chars, keep it under 50 chars.
- Branch name format: `<issue-number>-<friendly-name>` (e.g. `3-fix-login-bug`)
- Run: `git checkout -b <branch-name>`

### 5. Implement the changes
- Based on your analysis of the issue, make the necessary code changes.
- If the issue references specific files or code, read those files first.
- Make focused, minimal changes to address exactly what the issue describes.
- After implementation, run the project's lint/check commands if available.

### 6. Commit and push
- Stage all changes: `git add -A`
- Commit with a descriptive message referencing the issue (e.g. `fix: resolve login bug (#3)`)
- Push: `git push origin <branch-name>`

### 7. Create a Pull Request
- Run: `gh pr create --repo <owner/repo> --title "<commit message>" --body "Closes #<issue-number>" --base main`
- Show the PR URL to the user.

### 8. Wait for checks and fix if needed
- Poll for PR check status every 30 seconds using `gh pr view <number> --repo <owner/repo> --json statusCheckRollup`
- Wait until all checks complete (max 10 minutes).
- If any checks fail:
  - Read the failing check details to understand the issue.
  - Make the necessary fixes.
  - Commit and push again: `git add -A`, `git commit -m "fix: address review checks"`, `git push`
  - Wait again for checks to pass.
  - Repeat until all checks pass.

### 9. Merge the PR
- Once all checks pass, merge using: `gh pr merge <number> --repo <owner/repo> --squash --delete-branch`
- Verify the merge was successful.

### 10. Switch back to main and pull
- Run: `git switch main`
- Run: `git pull origin main`

Report the full summary to the user at the end.
